records.ety: [F., fr. L. porus, Gr. <?/ a passage, a pore. See Fare, v.]

defs: One of the minute orifices in an animal or vegetable membrane, for transpiration, absorption, etc.
A minute opening or passageway; an interstice between the constituent particles or molecules of a body; as, the pores of stones.

records.ety: [OE. poren, of uncertain origin; cf. D. porren to poke, thrust, Gael. purr.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Pored (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Poring.]

defs: To look or gaze steadily in reading or studying; to fix the attention; to be absorbed; -- often with on or upon, and now usually with over.
quotes: The eye grows weary with poring perpetually on the same thing.
